zotero.abstractNote: Value-based reinforcement learning (RL) methods often rely on a single value function to estimate the expected return. However, in many environments, the distribution of returns can be complex, multimodal, or heavy-tailed, making a single scalar estimate insufficient. Distributional RL addresses this by modeling the full distribution of returns. In this paper, we propose Orchestrated Value Mapping (OVM), a novel framework that learns a set of value functions, each specialized to a different aspect of the return distribution. By orchestrating these value functions, OVM can capture complex return distributions more effectively than existing methods. We demonstrate the effectiveness of OVM on a variety of challenging RL benchmarks, showing that it consistently outperforms state-of-the-art distributional RL algorithms.
